# Monitoring Stack Configuration Reference
# Generated by: setup_monitoring/create_cluster_monitoring.sh
# Date: Auto-generated

## PORTS OVERVIEW

### Valkey Cluster (setup_cluster)
  - Nodes: 127.0.0.1:7000 - 127.0.0.1:7005
  - Configuration: ../setup_cluster/cluster_config.sh

### Redis Exporters (oliver006/redis_exporter)
  - redis-exporter-7000: localhost:9200 → redis://127.0.0.1:7000
  - redis-exporter-7001: localhost:9201 → redis://127.0.0.1:7001
  - redis-exporter-7002: localhost:9202 → redis://127.0.0.1:7002
  - redis-exporter-7003: localhost:9203 → redis://127.0.0.1:7003
  - redis-exporter-7004: localhost:9204 → redis://127.0.0.1:7004
  - redis-exporter-7005: localhost:9205 → redis://127.0.0.1:7005

### Prometheus (prom/prometheus)
  - Port: 9090
  - URL: http://localhost:9090
  - Config file: ./prometheus.yml
  - Scrape targets: All redis-exporters (9200-9205)
  - Scrape interval: 15 seconds

### Grafana (grafana/grafana)
  - Port: 3000
  - URL: http://localhost:3000
  - Credentials: admin/admin
  - Config file: ./grafana.ini
  - Datasource: Prometheus (auto-configured)
  - Provisioning: ./provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml

## CONFIGURATION FILES

### prometheus.yml
Location: ./prometheus.yml
Purpose: Defines which metrics Prometheus scrapes
Contents:
  - Global settings (scrape interval: 15s)
  - Scrape configs for each redis-exporter
  - Instance labels for identification

### grafana.ini
Location: ./grafana.ini
Purpose: Main Grafana configuration
Key settings:
  - Server port: 3000
  - Admin password: admin
  - Database: SQLite (/var/lib/grafana/grafana.db)
  - Anonymous access: disabled

### provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml
Location: ./provisioning/datasources/prometheus.yml
Purpose: Auto-provision Prometheus as a datasource in Grafana
Configured for:
  - Name: Prometheus
  - URL: http://localhost:9090
  - Access: proxy
  - Default: yes

### provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml
Location: ./provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml
Purpose: Dashboard provisioning configuration
Function: Tells Grafana where to load dashboards from

## DATA DIRECTORIES

./data/
├── prometheus.yml                    # Prometheus config (auto-generated)
├── grafana.ini                       # Grafana config
├── provisioning/
│   ├── datasources/
│   │   └── prometheus.yml           # Prometheus datasource config
│   └── dashboards/
│       └── dashboards.yml           # Dashboard provisioning config
└── prometheus-storage/              # Prometheus time-series database

## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST

After starting the stack:

1. Check Valkey Cluster
   docker exec redis-exporter-7000 redis-cli -h 127.0.0.1 -p 7000 ping
   # Should respond: PONG

2. Check Redis Exporters
   curl http://localhost:9200/metrics
   # Should show redis_* metrics

3. Check Prometheus
   curl http://localhost:9090/-/healthy
   # Should respond: OK
   # Visit: http://localhost:9090/targets (check all targets Up)

4. Check Grafana
   curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
   # Should respond with health status
   # Visit: http://localhost:3000 (login admin/admin)

## ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES (Optional)

Override defaults by setting before running the script:

export EXPORTER_BASE_PORT=9200          # Base port for exporters
export PROMETHEUS_PORT=9090             # Prometheus port
export GRAFANA_PORT=3000                # Grafana port
export MONITORING_DATA_DIR=./config     # Config directory

Example:
PROMETHEUS_PORT=9999 GRAFANA_PORT=3333 \
  ./create_cluster_monitoring.sh /path/to/valkey start

## DOCKER NETWORKS

All services use --network host for simplicity:
- Allows direct localhost access between containers
- Simplifies port mapping
- Suitable for local development

## COMMON ISSUES

### Port Already in Use
Kill existing services:
  docker rm -f redis-exporter-* prometheus grafana

### Prometheus not scraping targets
1. Wait 15-30s for first scrape
2. Check http://localhost:9090/targets
3. Verify exporters are running: docker ps | grep redis-exporter
4. Check Prometheus config: http://localhost:9090/config

### Grafana datasource connection failed
1. Ensure Prometheus is running: docker ps | grep prometheus
2. Test connectivity: curl http://localhost:9090/-/healthy
3. In Grafana, test datasource: Connections → Data sources → Prometheus → Test

### Container won't start
1. Check Docker logs: docker logs [container-name]
2. Free up port: lsof -i :[port-number]
3. Ensure Docker daemon is running: docker ps
